artifacts.role: typed shell metadata for the compiler-emitted WP_HTTP_Response adapter
WPHX PHP compile fixture for wp-includes/class-wp-http-response.php
WPHX-312.52 oracle/candidate manifest using the compiler-emitted original-path public shell
behavior receipt for the retired temporary shell candidate

claims: WPHX PHP emits the original-path wp-includes/class-wp-http-response.php public class adapter for WP_HTTP_Response.
The WPHX-312.52 candidate no longer uses a generated temporary public PHP class shell for promoted response-object behavior.
PHP still owns the reflection-visible class ABI, public property serialization behavior, #[AllowDynamicProperties], and native absint boundary while Haxe owns the bounded response-state transitions.
non_claims: This does not claim REST server dispatch, Requests transport integration, or live HTTP behavior.
This does not claim installed WordPress distribution behavior or selected upstream HTTP PHPUnit parity for WP_HTTP_Response.
This does not claim broader response-object ownership or that WPHX PHP is a complete arbitrary-Haxe PHP backend.
